steps.title: Place Algeria in the argument
steps.content: The Algeria detail gives an example of a household-fuel shift: solid fuel use fell sharply. The text then says such shifts are often explained by the energy ladder, a model based mainly on household income. Heltberg's Ghana study is introduced to show that this model is reductive because fuel use can depend on several factors, including grid stability.

steps.title: Match the critique
steps.content: A wrongly says Algeria is poorly suited for evaluating the model; the passage uses it as the kind of change the model tries to explain. B invents two equally compelling explanations. C says Algeria and Ghana have similar causes, but the text uses Ghana to complicate the simple energy-ladder explanation. The answer is D.
